summary: OpportunityNotifier carries six canonical anti-patterns and is not promotable in its current shape. Two are P0/Critical: SOQL inside a for-loop on Opportunity (governor-limit time bomb at any bulk size > ~100 Opps with > 1 Contact each) and an HTTP callout to a hardcoded HTTPS endpoint instead of a Named Credential (operational + secrets risk). Two are P1/High: DML inside a nested for-loop (the inner Task insert) and `without sharing` on a class touching Opportunity/Contact (sharing-bypass risk for unprivileged callers). Two are P2/Medium: a swallowed Exception with no logging on the callout failure path, and a hardcoded RecordTypeId. Recommended remediation order: Named Credential first (unblocks the rewrite), then bulkify SOQL+DML, then flip to with-sharing, then add ApplicationLogger on the catch, then resolve RecordType by Developer Name via Schema.SObjectType.Task.getRecordTypeInfosByDeveloperName(). Do NOT modify the source in this pass — hand to apex-refactorer with this finding set.
confidence_rationale: Source is fully inlined and all six canonical signals are unambiguous in the static text; MEDIUM (not HIGH) because the harness has no live org probe to confirm whether the hardcoded RecordTypeId still resolves in the target org or whether the without-sharing keyword is intentional for a known internal caller.

findings.title: SOQL inside a for-loop on Opportunity
findings.detail: Inner Contact query runs once per Opportunity in the input list. At the 200-record trigger batch size that is 200 SOQL queries — 2x the 100-SOQL governor cap before the loop completes. The bug surfaces only at bulk and will not appear in single-record dev sandbox testing.
findings.recommendation: Lift the Contact query out of the loop. Build a Set<Id> accountIds from opps, run one bulk SOQL `SELECT Id, Email, AccountId FROM Contact WHERE AccountId IN :accountIds`, and key the result into a Map<Id, List<Contact>> for in-loop lookup. See `apex/bulkification` skill.

findings.title: Callout uses a hardcoded HTTPS endpoint
findings.detail: Apex constructs the endpoint inline and bypasses the Remote Site Settings/Named Credential governance surface. Credentials cannot be rotated without a code change, the endpoint is not subject to the org's auth-provider posture, and the URL contains a PII-leaking query parameter (`?email=`).
findings.recommendation: Provision a Named Credential (e.g. Notify_Webhook), move the path/query into the body or a path-only suffix, and change the request to `req.setEndpoint('callout:Notify_Webhook/notify')`. See `integration/named-credentials-setup` skill.

findings.title: Task insert inside the nested for-loop
findings.detail: Inner `insert t` fires once per (Opportunity × Contact) pair. At 200 Opps × 5 Contacts each that is 1000 DML statements vs the 150 governor cap.
findings.recommendation: Build a `List<Task> toInsert` inside the loop and run a single `insert toInsert;` after the loop ends. Combined with the SOQL bulkification this collapses the work to one SOQL + one DML.

findings.title: Class is `without sharing` while touching Opportunity / Contact
findings.detail: When invoked from an unprivileged context, the class will read Contact records the caller could not see directly and will write Tasks against records they could not own. This is a sharing-bypass surface.
findings.recommendation: Default to `with sharing`. If a system-mode pass is genuinely required (e.g. a queueable that runs as the integration user), isolate it in a single `without sharing` selector method and document the reason in the class header.

findings.title: Callout catch block swallows the exception with no logging
findings.detail: An empty catch on the HTTP send call means callout failures are invisible to ops — no Application_Log__c row, no debug log smell, no platform event. Production failures will look like silent success.
findings.recommendation: Replace the empty catch with a call to `ApplicationLogger.logException(e, 'OpportunityNotifier.notify', JSON.serialize(o))`. See `templates/apex/ApplicationLogger.cls`.

findings.title: Hardcoded RecordTypeId on Task
findings.detail: Hardcoded 18-char Id `0125g000000abcdAAA` will not resolve across orgs (it is per-org per-record-type) and silently breaks on sandbox refresh or org migration.
findings.recommendation: Resolve by Developer Name: `Id rtId = Schema.SObjectType.Task.getRecordTypeInfosByDeveloperName().get('Follow_Up').getRecordTypeId();`. Cache it in a static initializer if the class is hot.

deliverables.title: Code review — OpportunityNotifier with WAF pillar scores
deliverables.content:
# Code review — OpportunityNotifier.cls

**Verdict:** NOT PROMOTABLE. Two P0 + two P1 + two P2.

## Findings density
6 anti-patterns in 33 lines of source. The class is a portfolio of canonical Apex smells — prioritize remediation in the order below.

## Per-pillar Well-Architected scores
| Pillar | Score (1-5) | Driver |
|---|---|---|
| Reliable | 1 | SOQL/DML in loop = governor-limit time bomb at any bulk size; no fault path on the callout. |
| Secure | 1 | `without sharing` + hardcoded endpoint + PII in URL + no FLS. |
| Performant | 1 | O(N×M) SOQL + O(N×M) DML; will not survive the 200-row trigger batch. |
| Resilient | 2 | Swallowed exception means failures are invisible; no retry surface. |
| Cost-effective | 3 | No callout fan-out cost issue at single-record scale; degrades sharply at bulk. |
| Composable | 2 | No selector / service split; logic lives in one entrypoint that mixes I/O + business rules + side effects. |

## Remediation order (route to apex-refactorer)
1. **Named Credential first** — unblocks the rewrite without touching business logic.
2. **Bulkify SOQL** — single Contact query keyed on a Set<Id>.
3. **Bulkify DML** — collect Tasks, insert once after both loops collapse.
4. **Flip to `with sharing`** — remove the sharing-bypass surface.
5. **Add ApplicationLogger** — replace the empty catch.
6. **Resolve RecordType by Developer Name** — remove the hardcoded id.

## What this review did NOT do
- Did NOT modify the source code (read-only review).
- Did NOT generate the rewrite — see follow-up apex-refactorer.
- Did NOT generate tests — the rewrite must land first; then test-class-generator.

process_observations.observation: Two P0 findings co-located with two P1 findings in 33 lines of source is a smell about the upstream review process — either no PR review fired on this class, or the review missed the canonical anti-patterns. Worth flagging to the reviewing team.

The `without sharing` keyword may be intentional for a known internal callsite (e.g. a queueable run as the integration user). Without org context the reviewer cannot tell if the recommendation is correct or premature. Lowered confidence accordingly.
After remediation, regenerate the test class so callout tests stub through MockHttpResponseGenerator and the bulk path is asserted via Limits.getQueries() / getDmlStatements() deltas.
process_observations.followup_reason: because the rewritten class needs new tests covering the Named Credential mock, the bulk-safety assertion, and the with-sharing posture.

citations.used_for: Per-pillar Well-Architected scoring rubric and the canonical anti-pattern list (SOQL-in-loop, DML-in-loop, hardcoded endpoint, without-sharing, swallowed exception, hardcoded RecordTypeId).
callout:<NamedCredential> idiom for the hardcoded-endpoint remediation.
with-sharing default rubric and the FLS/CRUD posture for the SOQL/DML.

followups.because: because this review is read-only; the rewrite (Named Credential, bulk SOQL/DML, with-sharing flip, ApplicationLogger, RecordType-by-name) belongs in apex-refactorer.

because two of the findings are P0 security signals (hardcoded endpoint, without-sharing); a focused security-scanner pass should run before promotion to confirm no other security smells slipped through.
because the SOQL bulkification belongs behind a Selector layer with WITH USER_MODE; soql-optimizer owns the selector extraction.
because the class name suggests trigger-adjacent code; if this is invoked from a Task or Opportunity trigger, it should be consolidated into the canonical TriggerHandler pattern.
